note: U.S. hiring-compliance ruleset for HireGuard. Citations are real statutes/guidance. category_default is the starting severity; @RiskScorer and @Counsel may adjust based on evidence.

rules.title: Age-coded language (ADEA)
rules.citation: Age Discrimination in Employment Act, 29 U.S.C. § 623; EEOC age discrimination guidance
detection_hints: no more than X years experience
rules.recommendation_template: Remove age-proxy language. Describe required skills/competencies instead of age signals or graduation recency.

rules.title: Protected-class / gender-coded language (Title VII)
rules.citation: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, 42 U.S.C. § 2000e-2
rules.recommendation_template: Use neutral terms (e.g. 'salesperson', 'they'). Remove any reference to sex, race, religion, or national origin.

rules.title: National-origin / English-fluency overreach
rules.citation: Title VII; 29 C.F.R. § 1606.6 (national origin); EEOC English-only / fluency guidance
rules.recommendation_template: Replace 'native English speaker' with a job-related standard such as 'professional written and verbal English proficiency' only where the role requires it.

rules.title: Disability / physical-requirement overreach (ADA)
rules.citation: Americans with Disabilities Act, 42 U.S.C. § 12112; 29 C.F.R. § 1630
rules.recommendation_template: State physical demands only when they are essential job functions, and add that reasonable accommodations are available.

rules.title: Blanket criminal-history exclusion
rules.citation: EEOC Enforcement Guidance on Arrest and Conviction Records (2012); Title VII disparate impact
detection_hints: must pass background with no convictions
rules.recommendation_template: Avoid blanket criminal bars. Use an individualized assessment tied to the nature of the offense and job relevance; check applicable ban-the-box laws.

rules.title: Salary-history inquiry
rules.jurisdiction: Multi-state (e.g. CA, NY, MA, CO, WA)
rules.citation: CA Labor Code § 432.3(b); NY Labor Law § 194-a; and parallel state salary-history bans
rules.recommendation_template: Remove all requests for prior/current pay. Ask for salary expectations only, and post the range instead.

rules.title: Salary range disclosure required (California)
rules.citation: CA Labor Code § 432.3 (SB 1162, eff. 2023) — employers with 15+ employees must include pay scale in postings
rules.recommendation_template: Add the position's salary range (min–max) to the posting. Required for CA employers with 15+ employees.

rules.title: Salary range disclosure required (New York)
rules.citation: NY Labor Law § 194-b (eff. Sept 2023) — good-faith min/max compensation range in postings
rules.recommendation_template: Include a good-faith minimum and maximum salary/hourly range; required for NY employers with 4+ employees.

rules.title: Compensation + benefits disclosure (Colorado)
rules.citation: CO Equal Pay for Equal Work Act, C.R.S. § 8-5-201; 7 CCR 1103-13
rules.recommendation_template: Disclose the rate/range of pay AND a general description of benefits and other compensation; required for CO postings.

rules.title: Subjective scorecard criteria with disparate-impact risk
rules.citation: Title VII disparate-impact doctrine, 42 U.S.C. § 2000e-2(k); Griggs v. Duke Power Co. (1971)
rules.recommendation_template: Replace subjective 'culture fit' criteria with job-related, behaviorally-anchored competencies applied uniformly to all candidates.
